channel.title: The Grant Desk - Visual & Media Arts, added in last 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Visual & Media Arts, added in last 30 days.

item.title: Michele Larose - Osler Library Artist-in-Residence 2026-2027
item.description:
Organisation: Osler Library of the History of Medicine, McGill University (funded by Dr. Michele Larose)

Location: Montreal, Quebec, Canada (open to all; preference typically given to artists who reside in or can easily travel to Montreal)

Award: $6,000 CAD (taxes may be deducted); paid in two parts (half at the beginning of the residency, half on completion). Covers travel, materials, and related expenses. No application fee.

Duration: Research component flexible (2-3 weeks, consecutive or spread out) between October 2026 and April 2027; all work must be completed and the exhibition ready to open by 30 April 2027

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

Annual artist-in-residence award funded by Dr. Michele Larose, hosted by the Osler Library of the History of Medicine at McGill University, supporting visual artists who use McGill collections to create works addressing contemporary and/or historical subjects in medicine and the health sciences. Possible projects include painting, photography, performance, sculpture, and digital, video or installation art. Most work is anticipated to be inspired by Osler Library collections; sources may also include other McGill Libraries, the Maude Abbott Medical Museum, and McGill faculties and hospitals. NO STUDIO SPACE provided; the library offers research support, research space, and exhibition space. RESIDENCY DUTIES: work on the project; give a public presentation; exhibit or perform the work; submit a report suitable for the Osler Library Newsletter; complete the work by 30 April 2027. The artist retains ownership of the work but must credit Dr. Larose and the Osler Library. Applicants are encouraged to propose a project engaging one of the Faculty of Medicine and Health Sciences Strategic Research Plan themes: Infection (as a threat); Cancer (as a complex global challenge); The Brain; or Personalized/ing medicine. ELIGIBILITY: degree in Studio Arts or a related field and/or a history of exhibiting work in professional venues; open to non-Canadians, but preference typically given to Montreal-based or easily-traveling artists. APPLY by emailing ONE PDF (filename: lastname.LaroseOsler2026.pdf) to awards.oslerlibrary@mcgill.ca containing a 1-page project description, proposed timeline, CV, one letter of recommendation (addressed to Head Librarian Dr. Mary Hague-Yearl), and work samples (typically 5-15 images). Reference letters may also be sent separately by the referee before the deadline. Results announced in August or September. Deadline: 31 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: IMMA Dwell Here: One Year Studio Residency Open Call 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Irish Museum of Modern Art (IMMA)

Location: Dublin, Ireland (locally-based; commutable to IMMA; NO accommodation provided)

Award: Funded. EUR 4,000 bursary for the year; free access to an onsite IMMA studio; amenities (electricity, heating, Wi-Fi, parking); pre-agreed material/programming expenses related to the Research Assemblies. NOTE: EUR 10 application fee via Submittable. NO accommodation provided (apply to one of the other two Dwell Here calls if accommodation is needed).

Duration: Up to 11 months between January and December 2027 (start no earlier than 18 January 2027; all residencies finish by 15 December 2027)

Deadline: 22 Jun 2026

IMMA's Dwell Here One Year Studio Residency is the locally-based variant of the Dwell Here 2027 programme, supporting artists and thinkers commutable to IMMA in Dublin to undertake independent site-responsive research from an onsite studio (without accommodation). Open across visual arts, design, architecture, curation and related humanities fields. Structured around three week-long seasonal Research Assemblies in 2027 (Spring 03-09 March, Summer 09-15 June, Autumn 22-28 September), where residents engage with mentors, peers, fellow residents and the wider IMMA community through workshops, temporary exhibitions, reading groups, walking tours, open studios, talks, site visits, performances or other studio-related activities. RESEARCH THEMES (encouraged but not required): Technologies of Peace; The Irish Paradigm; The Museum as a Site of Vibration. ELIGIBILITY: artists and thinkers based within a reasonable commute to IMMA, committed to research excellence within their discipline. Notification September 2026. APPLY via Submittable as an Expression of Interest (EUR 10 fee). NOTE: there are two other Dwell Here open calls for 2027 (One Year Residency with accommodation, and a One Month Residency); applicants can only apply for ONE Dwell Here residency.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: IMMA Dwell Here: One Month Residency Open Call 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Irish Museum of Modern Art (IMMA)

Location: Dublin, Ireland (onsite at IMMA; open to worldwide applicants)

Award: Funded. EUR 250/week bursary for pre-agreed contracted weeks; free shared onsite accommodation (with own bedroom, separate to studio); access to a shared onsite IMMA studio (if required); amenities (electricity, heating, Wi-Fi, parking); one return journey for residents living outside Ireland. NOTE: EUR 10 application fee via Submittable. NOTE: cannot support accommodation for additional family members or partners.

Duration: One month (four weeks); three weeks of independent research plus one week that must overlap with a selected 2027 Research Assembly (Spring, Summer, or Autumn)

Deadline: 22 Jun 2026

IMMA's Dwell Here One Month Residency is the short-format variant of the Dwell Here 2027 programme, open to nationwide and internationally based artists and thinkers to undertake site-responsive research at IMMA in Dublin across visual arts, design, architecture, curation and related humanities fields. STRUCTURE: residents undertake three weeks of independent site-responsive research plus a fourth week (beginning, middle, or end) that must overlap with one of the three 2027 Research Assemblies (Spring 03-09 March, Summer 09-15 June, or Autumn 22-28 September). During the assembly week, residents engage with mentors, peers, fellow residents and the wider IMMA community through workshops, temporary exhibitions, reading groups, walking tours, open studios, talks, site visits, performances or other studio-related activities. Intent: deeper insight into the context of visual arts practices in Ireland, strengthening international connections and seeding ideas for proposed return residencies/programming. RESEARCH THEMES (encouraged but not required): Technologies of Peace; The Irish Paradigm; The Museum as a Site of Vibration. ELIGIBILITY: artists and thinkers based worldwide, committed to research excellence within their discipline. Self-catering facility; occasional hospitality. Notification October 2026. APPLY via Submittable (EUR 10 fee). NOTE: there are two other Dwell Here open calls for 2027 (One Year Residency with accommodation, and One Year Studio Residency for local applicants without accommodation); applicants can only apply for ONE Dwell Here residency.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Belgrade Art Studio: Virtual Art Lab (12 Weeks, All Media)
item.description:
Organisation: Belgrade Art Studio

Location: Remote (online; English-language program; worldwide)

Award: PAID PROGRAM (artist pays): EUR 675 program fee. NOT a grant: no stipend, no bursary, no production budget. Includes 12 one-hour 1-on-1 mentor sessions, a final online viewing/discussion, and artist interview/promotion. No separate application fee.

Duration: 12 weeks / 3 months; weekly 1-hour individual sessions (Zoom or Google Meet); not full-time

Deadline: Rolling / undated

PAID 12-week online mentorship program from Belgrade Art Studio: each artist works one-on-one with a dedicated artist-mentor through weekly 1-hour Zoom/Google Meet sessions, aimed at artists at a turning point in their practice (stuck, seeking new directions, experimenting with unfamiliar media including AI). Hybrid seminar/workshop format supporting both physical and online presentation, plus curatorial thinking, writing, interviews, and documentation. Closes with an online viewing and moderated discussion of the works produced. ELIGIBILITY: open worldwide to artists and creatives across visual arts, music, theatre, dance, film, writing, design, photography, digital/interactive art, games, fashion, architecture, and curation. English-language program (ability to collaborate in English preferred but accommodations possible). EXPECTATIONS: artists must treat the program as they would an in-person residency (dedicated time for research/creation/reflection), submit in-progress material for social-media exposure, participate in an artist interview, and refrain from hate speech or discriminatory content. APPLY by emailing CV, summary, and portfolio (links accepted) to artlab@belgradeartstudio.com. Rolling intake (no deadline). Selected artists are announced and contacted by email. COST: EUR 675 to participate — this is a paid offering, not a funded residency.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: OASis In the Park: Second Call (Parco San Laise, Naples) 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Fondazione Campania Welfare

Location: Parco San Laise, Bagnoli, Naples, Italy (former NATO base undergoing social/cultural regeneration)

Award: EUR 1,300 artist fee, plus travel, meals, accommodation, and production costs fully covered by the project. No application fee.

Duration: Approximately two weeks per selected artist, scheduled between September and October 2026

Deadline: 26 Jun 2026

Second round of OASis In the Park, Fondazione Campania Welfare's residency at Parco San Laise (a former NATO base in Bagnoli, Naples) following the first cycle (San Laise Open Lab). The OASis operates as a diffused platform across multiple spaces within the park, combining artistic creation, community engagement, and social activation. WHO: artists developing participatory, socially engaged practices with strong educational and/or community-focused components; particular encouragement for artists interested in working with children, youth, and local communities, though projects of broader social relevance to Bagnoli and community regeneration are welcome. DISCIPLINES (non-exhaustive): filmmaking and audiovisual media; photography; visual arts (sculpture, street art, graffiti, painting); textile, fashion and material practices; music and sound; AI-based artistic production; spatial design and installation art; lighting/light-based practices; interior and community-oriented design; illustration and graphic arts; multidisciplinary practice. ACTIVITIES: workshops, co-creation sessions, personal-but-community-based artistic projects, training initiatives, or other artistic interventions; focus refined during selection. SELECTION CRITERIA: artistic quality and originality; capacity for participatory and socially engaged practice; relevance to local context and community needs; clarity and concreteness of the proposal. APPLY by emailing fcw.oasis@gmail.com (subject line: 'OASis In the Park Application') with: CV (max 2 pages); portfolio (links, images, documentation); artistic project proposal (max 2 pages describing the project, community engagement plan, and intended outputs). TIMELINE: call closes 26 June 2026; artists notified July 2026; residency September-October 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Oceania Pacific Arts Residency 2026 (Suva, Fiji)
item.description:
Organisation: Creative New Zealand, Creative Australia, and the Oceania Centre at The University of the South Pacific (Suva, Fiji)

Location: Oceania Centre, The University of the South Pacific, Suva, Fiji

Award: NZD 9,000 artist fee; return travel to Suva, Fiji from New Zealand or Australia; accommodation and per diems during the residency; NZD 4,000 for project costs (insurance, materials, venue/equipment hire, promotion, ground transport, internet, local mentorship); networking and engagement opportunities. No application fee.

Duration: 6-week residency, 1 October to 12 November 2026

Deadline: 04 Jun 2026

Tri-nations pilot residency partnership between Creative New Zealand, Creative Australia, and the Oceania Centre at The University of the South Pacific in Suva, Fiji. Selects one established artist each from Aotearoa New Zealand, Australia, and Fiji for a shared 6-week residency at the Oceania Centre (1 October - 12 November 2026), supporting Pacific creatives to develop their practice and grow relationships and networks across Te Moana Nui a Kiwa. THEME: selected artists develop new work themed around Climate Change, a priority under Creative New Zealand's Pacific Arts Strategy and a pressing security issue for the peoples of the Pacific Islands. ACCOMMODATION: studio is suitable for a single artist or a couple; guests may be possible (discuss with the host before applying); additional access requirements can be accommodated on request. ELIGIBILITY: established artists and practitioners from New Zealand, Australia, and Fiji; specific eligibility criteria apply for each country (check the funder's page for your country). APPLY via the Creative New Zealand opportunities page. DEADLINE: 4 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: .ART Award 2026 (Global Art Prize)
item.description:
Organisation: .ART (with Whitewall Magazine, Chateau du Fresne, Anfitrion)

Location: Hybrid mobility (Grand Prize cash + residencies in France and Spain)

Award: Grand Prize: USD 15,000 cash. Artist Residency Award (France): one month at Chateau du Fresne, near Paris. Artist Residency Award (Spain): residency at Anfitrion in Marbella. Publication Award: editorial coverage in Whitewall Magazine (campaign media partner). .ART Domain Award: a USD 10,000 premium .ART domain name. No application fee mentioned (submission is via a .ART domain that documents the practice).

Duration: One-off open call; winners revealed during Art Basel Miami on 3 December 2026; residency prizes scheduled separately

Deadline: 01 Nov 2026

Global art prize celebrating how art is made, open to artists worldwide working in any medium or art form. Instead of a traditional application form, artists submit a digital presence (website, portfolio or social profile) linked through a .ART domain that documents identity, process and work as one continuous record. PRIZES: Grand Prize (USD 15,000 cash); Artist Residency Award France (one month at Chateau du Fresne, near Paris); Artist Residency Award Spain (residency at Anfitrion, Marbella); Publication Award (editorial coverage in Whitewall Magazine, the campaign's media partner); .ART Domain Award (USD 10,000 premium .ART domain name). ELIGIBILITY: individual artists and artist collectives from all backgrounds (cultures, ethnicities, gender identities, abilities); 18 or older at time of submission; open to artists from all geographic regions with no restrictions on nationality or place of residence. Multiple entries permitted, as long as each submission (single artwork, series or overall practice) is presented on or linked to a SEPARATE .ART domain. NOTE: a .ART domain is required for submission; this is the de facto entry mechanism. ANNOUNCEMENT: winners revealed during Art Basel Miami on 3 December 2026. DEADLINE: 1 November 2026. APPLY via the .ART Award site.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: CrafTopia Creative Hub: Material Dialogues Textile Waste Residency 2026 (Thessaloniki, Greece)
item.description:
Organisation: CrafTopia Creative Hub (under Culture Moves Europe, funded by the European Union and the Goethe-Institut)

Location: Thessaloniki, Greece

Award: Accommodation; shared working space; mentoring; networking opportunities; travel reimbursement; access to materials; administrative and logistical support; daily allowance (Culture Moves Europe standard). No application fee.

Duration: 30-day residency from 15 October to 13 November 2026

Deadline: 15 Jun 2026

CrafTopia Creative Hub, a Thessaloniki-based cultural and creative hub supporting artistic expression, learning and community engagement through residencies, workshops, exhibitions and collaborative projects, opens a Material Dialogues textile-waste residency for five visual artists. THEME: sustainability; the residency invites artists to work with textile waste sourced from industries operating in the same historic building as CrafTopia, emphasising the creative process over the final product. Participants are encouraged to experiment and collaborate, using their artistic skills in innovative ways with mixed media including paper and textile. ELIGIBILITY: visual artists who reside in eligible Culture Moves Europe countries; residency funded by the European Union and the Goethe-Institut under Culture Moves Europe. SUPPORT: accommodation, shared working space, mentoring, networking opportunities, travel reimbursement, access to materials, administrative/logistical support, and a daily allowance. RESIDENCY DATES: 15 October - 13 November 2026 (30 days). DEADLINE: 15 June 2026. APPLY via the CrafTopia open-call page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Khoj: Residency on Mental Health 2026 (New Delhi, India)
item.description:
Organisation: Khoj Studios (in collaboration with OAS Center for Anthropology and Mental Health Research in Action)

Location: Khoj Studios, New Delhi, India

Award: Travel to and from Delhi; a per-diem; and a production budget. One UK artist additionally supported by OAS's Center for Anthropology and Mental Health Research in Action. No application fee.

Duration: On-site residency from 3 August to 16 September 2026

Deadline: 07 Jun 2026

Khoj Studios invites Indian and international artists whose practice engages with mental health and distress, critically examining how pain (physical, psychological, or historical) shapes both the self and society. On-site residency at Khoj Studios in New Delhi, 3 August - 16 September 2026. THEMATIC FRAME: how is suffering unequally distributed across race, caste, class, gender, and geography? How do individuals and communities navigate, resist, or reclaim forms of vitality in the face of war, colonial legacies, and systemic inequalities? Rather than defining mental health and illness solely in medical terms, the residency is open to a range of knowledge systems, forms of practice, and diverse concepts of vitality and distress. ENGAGES: clinical and everyday experiences; myth and ritual as alternative or mainstream ways to process pain; ancestors, spirits, oral histories, and expressive traditions as tools of healing; digital cultures, new technologies, and clinical subjectivities reshaping the self, anxiety, coping, memory and aspiration. Brings together artists and scholars from India and the UK to explore varied forms of vitality and distress shaping contemporary life-worlds. ELIGIBILITY: Indian and international artists working in any medium. SUPPORT: travel to/from Delhi, per-diem, production budget; one UK artist additionally supported by OAS's Center for Anthropology and Mental Health Research in Action. DEADLINE: 7 June 2026. APPLY via the Khoj International Residency page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Aerowaves Twenty27: Call for Emerging European Choreographers
item.description:
Organisation: Aerowaves (Spring Forward festival hosted in Limerick, Ireland)

Location: Festival in Limerick, Ireland (14-17 April 2027) plus partner venues across Europe; applicants must be resident in geographical Europe

Award: For presentation at Spring Forward: a fee plus expenses including travel within Europe, hotel and a per diem/hospitality for each team member. Around 100 partner performance opportunities are guaranteed each year and supported by Aerowaves. Selected applicants additionally receive a year of Aerowaves promotion (artist profile, images, video, press coverage). No application fee.

Duration: One year of Aerowaves promotion and partner-network presentations; presentation at Spring Forward 14-17 April 2027

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

Aerowaves selects 20 emerging choreographers based in geographical Europe each year (the Twenty27 cohort) to have their work presented at the Spring Forward festival (14-17 April 2027 in Limerick, Ireland) and at many of Aerowaves' partners around Europe; Aerowaves promotes the selected artists for a year, creating performance opportunities with partners. EVERY APPLICANT (selected or not) has the chance to be programmed by Aerowaves network partners; around 100 performance opportunities per year are supported by Aerowaves. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must be resident in geographical Europe; due to the Russian invasion of Ukraine, Aerowaves partners do NOT accept applications of works made in Russia or Belarus. ONE work per applicant per year. The work must have been produced in geographical Europe; must be 15-40 minutes; must be finished and have been presented to the public (a studio sharing counts; work-in-progress or rehearsal footage does NOT). The work should be easily included in a double or triple bill with simple technical requirements; works created for public/non-conventional spaces and outdoors are welcome. WHAT SELECTED ARTISTS GET: presentation fee at Spring Forward plus travel (within Europe), hotel and per diem/hospitality for each team member; a year of Aerowaves website promotion. DEADLINE: 15 July 2026. APPLY via the Aerowaves how-to-apply page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: FOMAS Group Special Prize 2026 (within Arte Laguna Prize)
item.description:
Organisation: FOMAS Group (in collaboration with Arte Laguna Prize)

Location: Italy (FOMAS HQ Lecco; presented within Arte Laguna at Arsenale Nord, Venice)

Award: First prize EUR 7,000; second EUR 4,000; third EUR 3,000 (all taxes included). NOTE: works are entered via the Arte Laguna Prize, which has a registration fee (approx. EUR 97-122). The winning works become FOMAS Group property (rights of economic use, reproduction, communication to the public, exhibition, distribution and digitisation transferred to FOMAS).

Duration: One-off special prize awarded within Arte Laguna Prize 2026

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Special Prize from FOMAS Group, an Italian multinational in forging/rolling/metal powders, awarded within the Arte Laguna Prize 2026. THEME: 'the journey of steel in its crossing from hot to cold' - a metamorphosis of matter from incandescent casting to a cooled, smooth, precise surface; works should evoke this transition. FORMAT: hangable panel or canvas, dimensions between 1.5x1.5 m (minimum) and 3x2 m (maximum); unconventional materials encouraged. THREE AWARDS to nominated works: EUR 7,000 first, EUR 4,000 second, EUR 3,000 third (taxes included), paid after the work is presented. IMPORTANT CONDITION: the winning works become the property of FOMAS Group, with the artists ceding ownership and rights of economic use, reproduction, communication to the public, exhibition, distribution and digitisation for institutional and promotional purposes. APPLY by submitting via the Arte Laguna Prize portal (which has a registration fee, approx. EUR 97-122 for the full prize). Registration for the FOMAS Special Award closes 1 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Artadia Awards 2026: San Francisco Bay Area
item.description:
Organisation: Artadia

Location: San Francisco Bay Area, California, United States

Award: Three Awardees each receive a US$15,000 unrestricted award. Finalists not chosen as Awardees receive an honorarium. Awardees also access the Artadia Network and the Online Artist Registry. No application fee.

Duration: One-off award; two-round jury (application review + virtual studio visits)

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Artadia's open call for the San Francisco Bay Area, providing unrestricted financial support, exposure and recognition to contemporary visual artists. Three Awardees receive $15,000 each (use freely); Finalists not chosen as Awardees receive an honorarium. Awardees join the Artadia Network and get a dedicated webpage on the Artadia Online Artist Registry. PROCESS: open call (May 1 - June 1, 2026); first-round jury of three curators (one local) selects six Finalists; second-round jury conducts 45-minute virtual studio visits; three Awardees announced. ELIGIBILITY: contemporary visual artist making work for a contemporary-art context; living and working in the Bay Area's eligible counties and resident there for 2+ consecutive years prior to the deadline; not currently enrolled (or planning to enrol) in an art-related degree programme; have never received an Artadia/Art Council award of $10,000 or more (former finalists may re-apply). Apply via Submittable only.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Naomi Anolic Early Career Jewish Visual Arts Award 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Anolic Family Award

Location: Online application (open to early-career Jewish artists)

Award: $1,000 award for one early-career Jewish artist. No application fee.

Duration: One-off award; project supported within the award year, with a final artist statement and visual documentation due at year end

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Annual award providing momentum for early-career Jewish visual artists for a specific project. The 2026 cycle focuses on the fine arts: painting, drawing, sculpture and mixed media. Recipients must provide a brief artist statement and visual documentation of the supported work at year end, and must credit the Naomi Anolic Early Career Jewish Visual Arts Award in any exhibition or publication of the supported work. ELIGIBILITY: active early-career Jewish artists aged 25 to 35; students may not apply. APPLY with a max 600-word proposal (why you're applying, why you're early-career, project/focus, how the award will help, how Judaism informs and is realised in your work - overt Jewish imagery is not required), 10 jpg images (max 1024px longest side, max 4 MB each), an image list, an up-to-2-page CV/resume, and an artist website (or social-media link if no site). Award announcement by September 2026. Questions: info@anolicfamilyaward.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Gasworks Residency 2026: Artists Based in Greece
item.description:
Organisation: Gasworks (London)

Location: Gasworks, London, United Kingdom

Award: Fully funded 11-week residency: 24/7 private studio at Gasworks, single-room accommodation in Gasworks Residency House, economy return flights + taxi transfers from home country, all visa costs and visa-application support, weekly stipend of GBP 175 for living expenses, materials budget up to GBP 800, prepaid TFL Zones 1+2 travel card for the residency, studio visits with staff/curators/artists, an Open Studio event and presentation, dedicated residency page on the Gasworks website with a short video interview, networking and at least two individual studio visits, plus admin/pastoral/curatorial support. No application fee.

Duration: 11 weeks: 30 September - 15 December 2026

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Gasworks residency open call for a contemporary visual artist at a pivotal point in their career, based in Greece. The 11-week, fully funded residency takes place at Gasworks in London 30 September - 15 December 2026. The programme supports professional development, cultural exchange and artistic experimentation; up to 20 residencies per year, self-led, non-prescriptive and process-based. Selection panel includes Gasworks reps + external advisors with specialist knowledge of the Greek and UK contemporary art scenes; a shortlist of four artists is invited to a short online interview before the final decision. ELIGIBILITY: contemporary visual artists (not duos/collectives) based in Greece, with at least a moderate level of spoken English; artists studying outside Greece may apply if they have a permanent address in Greece and will be living there during the residency dates. Priority is given to artists who have not previously worked or studied in London. APPLY: one PDF (max 15MB) with cover sheet, 250-word practice statement, 250-word residency-plan statement, up to 15 images or video/sound clips of recent work (with captions), CV (max 3 pages), and any relevant documentation; spoken responses can substitute the written statements via embedded video/audio links. Deadline 1 June 2026, 1pm UK time. Supported by Thalia and Alex Haidas.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Gasworks Residency 2026: Artists Based in Aotearoa New Zealand
item.description:
Organisation: Gasworks (London), with the Office for Contemporary Art Aotearoa

Location: Gasworks, London, United Kingdom

Award: Fully funded 11-week residency: 24/7 private studio at Gasworks, single-room accommodation in Gasworks Residency House, economy return flights + taxi transfers from home country (1 cabin + 1 hold bag), all visa costs and visa-application support, weekly stipend of GBP 175, materials budget up to GBP 800, prepaid TFL Zones 1+2 travel card, studio visits, an Open Studio event on Saturday 5 December 2026, a dedicated residency page on the Gasworks website with short video interview, networking + at least two individual studio visits, plus admin/pastoral/curatorial support. No application fee.

Duration: 11 weeks: 30 September - 15 December 2026

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Gasworks residency open call for an early-career contemporary visual artist based in Aotearoa New Zealand. The 11-week, fully funded residency takes place at Gasworks in London 30 September - 15 December 2026. Self-led, non-prescriptive, process-based programme supporting professional development, cultural exchange and experimentation. Selection by panel of Gasworks staff and external advisors with specialist knowledge of the Aotearoa NZ and UK contemporary-art scenes; priority for artists who have not yet worked in London; shortlist of four invited to a short online interview (week beginning 22 June 2026); successful applicant notified the week beginning 6 July 2026. ELIGIBILITY: early-career contemporary visual artists based in Aotearoa NZ only; at least moderate spoken English; no duos/collectives, no accompanying partners/children supported. APPLY: one PDF (max 15MB) with cover sheet, 250-word practice statement, 250-word residency-plan statement, up to 15 images/video/sound clips of recent work (captions, up to 100 words per work), CV (max 3 pages), and any relevant documentation; spoken responses via embedded video/audio links accepted. Deadline 1 June 2026, 1pm UK time. Supported by the Jan Warburton Charitable Trust and Aotearoa NZ Friends of Gasworks, with additional support from the British Council Aotearoa NZ and the Pacific, in partnership with the Office for Contemporary Art Aotearoa.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Light Work: Artist-in-Residence 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Light Work

Location: Syracuse, New York, United States

Award: $7,500 stipend plus furnished housing and 24-hour access to Light Work's digital lab, studios and equipment. No application fee stated; check before applying.

Duration: One-month residency (one of several months across 2027)

Deadline: 01 Jul 2026

One-month artist-in-residence programme at Light Work in Syracuse, New York, for artists working in photography or image-based media. Residents receive a $7,500 stipend, furnished apartment-style accommodation, 24-hour access to Light Work's lab and equipment, and editorial/curatorial engagement (publication, exhibition, archival inclusion). ELIGIBILITY: open to artists from any country working in photography or image-based media; current students are not eligible. Submit via SlideRoom. Applications for the 2027 cycle are open through 1 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Malakta x Kulturhuset Dux: Community Art Commission and Residency 2026 (Kristinestad)
item.description:
Organisation: Art in Malakta rf (with Kulturhuset Dux and Kristinestad Artist Residency)

Location: Kristinestad / Malax, Finland

Award: EUR 6,000 artist fee plus EUR 5,000 materials budget, with travel and residency accommodation included. No application fee.

Duration: One-month residency 1 Sep - 4 Oct 2026 (Phase 1) plus production phase, with artwork completed by summer 2027 (Phase 2)

Deadline: Closed 31 May 2026

Commission and residency from Art in Malakta rf with Kulturhuset Dux and Kristinestad Artist Residency to create a public artwork for Kulturhuset Dux or its immediate surroundings, in dialogue with Kristinestad and its residents. The medium is open (installation, object, sound, textile, light, digital); what matters is the relationship between the work, the site and the people. The project is rooted in Kristinestad's identity as a Cittaslow town and as Baltic Sea Cultural Pearl 2026. PHASE 1 (1 Sep - 4 Oct 2026): a one-month residency at Kristinestad Artist Residency for research, meetings, workshops, and artistic development. PHASE 2: production after the residency (remotely or at Malakta if needed); the artwork must be completed by summer 2027 and requires on-site presence. ELIGIBILITY: open to artists or artist collectives wanting to work site-specifically and in dialogue with residents. APPLY by email to communityart@malakta.fi by 31 May 2026 with a motivation letter, portfolio, CV and website link as a single PDF (max 5 MB). Interviews before 19 June 2026.

Open call details

item.title: VH AWARD 2026 (Hyundai Motor Group)
item.description:
Organisation: Hyundai Motor Group

Location: Online residency (global exhibitions); for artists engaging with Asia

Award: Each of five finalists receives a USD 30,000 production grant plus an Ars Electronica online residency; the Grand Prix winner receives an additional USD 30,000 (USD 60,000 total). Honorary Mentions join selected residency components. No application fee.

Duration: Online residency Sep 2026 - Mar 2027 (finalists); award ceremony and exhibitions June 2027

Deadline: 21 Jul 2026

The VH AWARD, hosted by Hyundai Motor Group, supports emerging media artists whose work engages with the context of Asia, through an online residency and exhibitions across global platforms. FIELD: audiovisual, screen-based artworks including video art, motion graphics, animation, games, films or newly explored areas; proposals must suit single-channel video presentation (no interactivity). ELIGIBILITY: individuals or collectives whose work engages with the context of Asia, including artists of Asian descent, those based in Asia, and the Asian diaspora. Five finalists are selected, each receiving a USD 30,000 production grant and a place in the Ars Electronica online residency program (masterclasses and 1:1 mentoring), with networking and exhibition opportunities; one Grand Prix recipient (announced 2027) receives an additional USD 30,000; a new Honorary Mention category recognises one or two further applicants with website features and selected residency components. APPLY: via the VH AWARD website Entry Portal with a video portfolio (max 3 min), CV and artist statement (max 3 pages), and a new artwork proposal (max 3 pages) with a reference video (max 1 min); all documents in English; proposed works must be original and unexhibited. Open call 26 May - 21 July 2026 (KST); finalists announced September 2026.

item.title: DARC Indigenous Residency 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Digital Arts Resource Centre (DARC), with The Hnatyshyn Foundation

Location: Ottawa-Gatineau, Canada (must reside within commutable distance)

Award: CAD $2,500 artist fee, plus $520 for an artist talk, $1,504 for a chosen artistic/Indigenous advisor (16 hrs), up to $4,000 in DARC equipment/facility waivers, transit/parking honorarium for local artists, and for non-local artists up to $1,500 housing subsidy and $1,750 travel. Plus an Asinabka festival pass and one year of DARC membership/workshops. No application fee.

Duration: One-month on-site residency, 1-31 October 2026

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

One-month intensive on-site residency at the Digital Arts Resource Centre in Ottawa, presented with support from The Hnatyshyn Foundation, for mid-career Indigenous (First Nations, Inuit and Metis) artists developing their practice, experimenting with a new medium, or continuing a project. Residents get access to DARC's Microcinema, Soundstage, Digital Edit Suite and Recording Studio, audio-visual equipment, and up to 16 hours of advisor time. Proposals are welcome across film, video, animation, web-based art, sound art, AR/VR, interactive and time-based digital projects, and media-art installations, in a collaborative environment encouraging hands-on technological play. A public artist talk follows the residency (by 19 December 2026). ELIGIBILITY: mid-career Indigenous artists (a consistent body of work, publicly presented in a professional setting); must be a DARC Extended Access member (free membership granted on applying); must reside in the Ottawa-Gatineau area within commutable distance of DARC for the on-site residency. Priority is given to Indigenous artists who also identify as IBPOC and/or 2SLGBTQIA+. Working languages English and French. Info session 10 June 2026; application deadline 30 June 2026; selection by an Indigenous recommender jury.

Open call details

item.title: IMMA Dwell Here: One Year Residency Open Call 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Irish Museum of Modern Art (IMMA)

Location: Dublin, Ireland (onsite at IMMA)

Award: Funded. Monthly EUR 1,000 bursary for pre-agreed contracted months; free onsite accommodation (separate to studio); access to an onsite IMMA studio (if required); amenities (electricity, heating, Wi-Fi, parking); one return journey for residents living outside Ireland; pre-agreed material/programming expenses related to the Research Assemblies. NOTE: EUR 10 application fee via Submittable.

Duration: Up to 11 months between January and December 2027 (start no earlier than 18 January 2027; all residencies finished by 15 December 2027)

Deadline: 22 Jun 2026

IMMA's Dwell Here One Year Residency offers research-led residencies in Dublin across the visual arts, design, architecture, curation and related humanities fields, supporting independent site-responsive research. Structured around three week-long seasonal Research Assemblies in 2027 (Spring 03-09 March, Summer 09-15 June, Autumn 22-28 September) where residents engage with mentors, peers, fellow residents and the wider IMMA community through workshops, temporary exhibitions, reading groups, walking tours, open studios, talks, site visits, performances or other studio-related activities. RESEARCH THEMES (encouraged but not required): Technologies of Peace (commemorative landscapes, memories of peace, sustainable coexistence); The Irish Paradigm (Ireland as small island on the edge of Europe, periphery vs centre); The Museum as a Site of Vibration (museum and site within the built legacy of empire, erased/censored/marginalised histories, planetary care, hospitality). ELIGIBILITY: artists and thinkers based in Ireland or internationally, committed to research excellence within their discipline; framed around research excellence rather than career stage. Some onsite accommodation suitable for partners or small families (discuss with the residency programmer); self-catering facility. Notification September 2026. APPLY via Submittable (EUR 10 fee). NOTE: there are two other Dwell Here open calls for 2027 (additional one-year and one-month residencies); applicants can only apply for ONE Dwell Here residency.

Open call details

item.title: Bayt AlMamzar x Scanteia: Bucharest Residency Exchange 2026-2027
item.description:
Organisation: Bayt AlMamzar (BAM) and Scanteia

Location: Residency in Bucharest, Romania (applicants must be UAE-based)

Award: Funded residency: private ~50 sqm studio with accommodation at Atelierele Scanteia, flight covered up to $500, $500 artist fee, local transport card, access to large-format inkjet printing, photo studio, wood workshop and scanners, plus a curated joint exhibition. No application fee.

Duration: One month (Residency 1: Aug-Sep 2026; Residency 2: mid-March to mid-April 2027), with a joint exhibition end of April 2027

Deadline: 07 Jun 2026

An international exchange between Bayt AlMamzar (BAM), an artist-run space in Dubai, and Scanteia, an artist-run space in Bucharest, inviting UAE-based artists to undertake individual month-long residencies in Bucharest across 2026-2027, followed by a joint exhibition curated by Scanteia. Two artists are selected for non-overlapping residencies (Aug-Sep 2026 and mid-March to mid-April 2027). Scanteia provides a private ~50 sqm studio with live-in accommodation, a flight subsidy up to $500, a $500 artist fee, a local transport card, weekly studio visits with Romanian curators and gallerists, study trips (MARe, MNAC, EVA Foundation), access to specialised equipment (large-scale inkjet printing, photography studio, wood workshop, scanners), exhibition space, and documentation/publication support via BAM's publishing arm BAMBAM. ELIGIBILITY: open to visual artists, researchers and practitioners currently based in the UAE, aged 18+, at any career stage; a valid UAE residence visa is required at the time of application. No nationality restriction, but applicants without an EU or Emirati passport will need a Schengen visa (Scanteia provides an invitation letter). They seek artists with a developed practice and a specific, articulable reason to spend time in Bucharest (exploration, research, production or exchange), with no fixed theme or medium. Deadline 7 June 2026. Contact hello@baytalmazmar.org.

Open call details

item.title: FPP Residency & Exhibition 2026 (Konstkollektivet / Flower Power Photography)
item.description:
Organisation: Konstkollektivet, with Flower Power Photography, Centrum for fotografi and Artist in Coexistence (AiC)

Location: Orust and Molndal, Sweden

Award: Artist fee 7,000 SEK plus exhibition fee 3,400 SEK, travel support up to 4,000 SEK (slow travel by train/bus encouraged), and accommodation for the whole period. Plus facilitation, mentorship and research support. No application fee. (Amounts in SEK, incl. VAT where applicable.)

Duration: 14-day residency 3-17 August 2026; group exhibition opens 1 September 2026

Deadline: 14 Jun 2026

Residency and exhibition for visual artists whose practice engages with experimental, alternative and/or critical image-making (analogue processes, archives or hybrid approaches), with a particular interest in image-making as a way of living and thinking alongside the planet and other beings, embracing slowness, collaboration and more-than-human care. Organised by Konstkollektivet with Flower Power Photography (an artistic research project by Sam McCarthy and Lasse Lindqvist on non-violence, ecology and more-than-human ethics), Centrum for fotografi, and Artist in Coexistence (AiC). Includes a 14-day residency on the island of Orust (3-15 August), a process presentation, a workshop contribution at Konstkollektivet (15-16 August), and participation in a group exhibition at Konstparken opening 1 September, plus accommodation, an artist fee (7,000 SEK), exhibition fee (3,400 SEK), and travel support up to 4,000 SEK. ELIGIBILITY: European-based visual artists of all ages and nationalities. APPLICATION: one PDF (max 10MB) with a project proposal/motivation (max 1 page), artist statement (max 150 words), CV (1 page) and portfolio (max 5 pages) to residens@konstkollektivet.se, subject line 'Name_LastName_FPP_Residency', by 14 June 2026.

Open call details

item.title: PhotoVogue Global Open Call 2026: Brave New Visions - Creativity as Rebellion
item.description:
Organisation: PhotoVogue (Conde Nast)

Location: Online (global)

Award: $12,000 in total grants split across three artists: a $6,000 Outstanding Vision Grant, a $4,000 Vision Grant, and a $2,000 Rising Voice Grant (emerging), plus presentation at the next PhotoVogue Festival, potential publication across Vogue's global editions, and PhotoVogue Virtual Portfolio Reviews. Free to submit.

Duration: One-off open call

Deadline: 11 Sep 2026

PhotoVogue's global open call invites photographers and video makers to use image-making as a form of rebellion - to challenge indifference, disrupt conventions and expand visual storytelling. It asks not for a theme but for a position in relation to the world. Open to photography, video and multimedia projects across all genres (fashion, documentary, portraiture, fine art, experimental). Three artists share $12,000 in grants ($6,000 Outstanding Vision, $4,000 Vision, $2,000 Rising Voice for an emerging artist), with festival presentation, potential Vogue publication and portfolio reviews. ELIGIBILITY: open to all artists worldwide aged 18+; submissions are free; previous PhotoVogue applicants may submit a new project. SUBMIT: a series of up to 15 images, or images plus video for multimedia, and/or a 60-second trailer, via Picter. IMPORTANT: AI-generated works are NOT eligible. Open 14 May to 11 September 2026 (11:59 PM CET).

Open call details

item.title: Lanesboro Arts: Early Career Artist Residency 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Lanesboro Arts

Location: Lanesboro, Minnesota, United States (pop. 754)

Award: $1,250 weekly stipend (fixed per residency, including for small collaborating teams); housing provided. No application fee.

Duration: Two or four week residency, scheduled within February 1 to December 31, 2027

Deadline: Closed 30 May 2026

Residency program in the rural community of Lanesboro, MN, supported by the Jerome Foundation, offering early career artists two or four week residencies to explore how their work can engage the community and how a rural community can inform their work. Staff co-create a fully customized residency experience around community engagement. Lanesboro Arts offers two to three residencies per year; the program provides an individual studio plus access to conference rooms, desk space, a gallery, piano and stage, with private housing (apartment, kitchen, private room and bathroom). No meals provided. Open to all disciplines. ELIGIBILITY: legal residents of Minnesota or one of the five boroughs of New York City for at least one year prior to applying; defined as early career artists with at least a 2-year and no more than 10-year track record of creating and presenting their own projects (not first-time creators or beginning artists). Degree-seeking students at the time of application or during the grant period (K-12, college, graduate or postgraduate) are not eligible. Age is not a factor. The primary goal must be to generate new work rather than remount or re-interpret existing work. Apply via the online webform on the Lanesboro Arts website. Selection announced June 9, 2026.

item.title: ICCI ART VALLEY: International Visiting Art Scholars 2026
item.description:
Organisation: USC-SJTU Institute of Cultural and Creative Industry (ICCI), Shanghai Jiao Tong University

Location: Shanghai, China

Award: Minimum RMB 8,000 one-time project grant (for production and lecture/workshop materials), plus round-trip economy airfare from the artist's departure city, accommodation, and group-exhibition installation costs. Note: a one-time RMB 8,000 program fee applies. Requires donating two artworks to Shanghai Jiao Tong University's permanent collection.

Duration: Residency November 1 to December 15, 2026 (full commitment required)

Deadline: 14 Jul 2026

International visiting artist/scholar residency at the USC-SJTU Institute of Cultural and Creative Industry in Shanghai, recruiting 5-6 participants for a cross-media, cross-cultural and cross-spatial exploration of contemporary art at a time when AI and spatial computing are reshaping artistic expression. Three program directions: Art and Technology (digital and material interaction); Humanistic Dialogue (cross-cultural understanding through exchange); and Urban Engagement (responding to urban transformation). Residents receive an individual studio plus access to ceramics, computer lab, sculpture, sound, VR and print facilities, a library and gallery, and private apartment housing (no meals). Support includes 1-2 assistants, organized museum and gallery visits, exchange with local Chinese artists, a group exhibition, and media promotion. ELIGIBILITY: teaching or public-lecture/seminar experience at a university or art institution; an active artistic practice (priority to interdisciplinary media, digital art, easel painting or integrated media); ability to organize 1-2 public events (lectures or workshops) during the residency; fluent English presentation and strong cross-cultural communication skills. Selected artists must donate two artworks for SJTU's permanent collection (provide a list of at least five works), actively participate in ICCI activities, and fully commit to the residency period. Selection announced July 30, 2026. Working languages: English and Mandarin.

Open call details

item.title: Eloquence Art Prize 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Power Institute, University of Sydney (Culture+)

Location: Australia (participating universities)

Award: National public speaking prize (award for the winning art talk on French and Australian cultural ties). No application fee.

Duration: Three-stage competition during 2026 (university shortlist, recorded talk, national live final)

Deadline: Closed 31 May 2026

Annual national public speaking challenge for students at participating Australian universities, rewarding the best art talk on French and Australian cultural ties. The 2026 theme is 'Art and Environment, France and Australia: Our forests and trees in danger': students compare two artists, one Australian and one French (at least one a visual artist), analysing how a sensibility to nature shaped their work on forests and trees from the 19th century to the present. THREE STAGES: (1) submit a 400-word summary paper outlining a proposed 7-minute talk; university panels shortlist three applicants; (2) shortlisted applicants submit a recording of the full 7-minute talk and panels select one representative per university; (3) representatives compete in a live (or recorded) national final. Stage 1 summaries are judged on compliance with instructions (10%), quality of sources (15%), relevance to theme (15%), writing and structure (20%), and interest/depth/originality (40%). ELIGIBILITY: students in Arts or Humanities disciplines; in the final year of a Bachelor degree, or Honours, Graduate Certificate, Masters or PhD students; enrolled for the first semester of the year at a participating institution; over 18. Stage 1 due 11:59PM, 31 May 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

Location: Paris, France (residency); applicants must be Australian

Award: $13,000 AUD towards travel and living costs, plus rent-free access to the Power Institute's living/working studio at the Cite Internationale des Arts for 3 months. Note: fellows pay a refundable bond deposit and utility costs. No application fee.

Duration: 3-month residency in one quarterly period (Jan-Mar, Apr-Jun, Jul-Sep, or Oct-Dec)

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

Fellowships for Australian artists to live and work at the Cite Internationale des Arts in Paris for three months, undertaking a specific artistic project using the city's institutions, exhibitions, archives, libraries, collections and artistic resources. Four fellowships are offered per year. Fellows receive rent-free access to the Power Institute's dedicated living/working studio at the Cite, $13,000 AUD towards travel and living costs (paid roughly 6 weeks before travel), and access to the Cite's facilities and global community of artists plus the broader Paris art infrastructure. Fellows pay a refundable bond and utility costs, and on return must share outcomes via a report, public event and/or exhibition. ELIGIBILITY: open to all artists who are Australian citizens or Permanent Residents; applicants cannot have previously held a Cite Internationale des Arts residency (via the Power Institute or any other organisation). Awarded by a committee chaired by the Director of the Power Institute in consultation with the Cite, judged on the strength of the project, the benefit of pursuing it via a Paris residency, and the potential to further the applicant's professional development. Applications open 20 May 2026 and close midnight, 30 June 2026; applicants notified September 2026.

item.title: KAMP: Creative Body Institute Residency, Lesbos, Greece 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Creative Body Institute (CBI)

Location: Lesbos Island, Greece (eco-art hermitage in an olive grove on Mount Lepetymnos)

Award: Co-funded residency. Total cost per artist EUR 2,500; CBI funds EUR 1,350, so the artist contribution is EUR 1,150 (plus a EUR 100 non-refundable deposit deducted from that contribution). Does not include travel, visas, airport transfers, insurance or personal food. One full contribution waiver is available per cohort (request on the form; reviewed after selection, based on financial need and cohort balance).

Duration: Roughly 3-week residency per track; tracks run July 22 to September 26, 2026

Deadline: Closed 24 May 2026

KAMP is a communal residency from the artist-run non-profit Creative Body Institute, inviting queer-identifying artists to an ecological hermitage on Lesbos Island, Greece, to develop work exploring queer ecologies, queer temporality, and ecofeminist practices through a human and more-than-human, life-art ethos. CBI seeks artists whose practices engage embodiment, interdependence and temporality through performance, installation, sound, dance or experimental visual work. FOUR TRACKS: (1) Experimental Media and Performativity, Jul 22-Aug 10; (2) Relational and Site-Responsive Work, Aug 14-Sep 2; (3) Body-Based and Durational Practice, Sep 6-24/26; (4) Writer in Residence (dates coinciding with one of the above, for those focused on exegesis, a publication, or writing about prior embodied/artistic research). 5-7 artists are selected per cohort. Housing is simple cabin-based accommodation for relational living (Queer Family Cabin for 2-3, Cozy Cabin for solitude, Sanctuary Cabin for 2 sharing), with communal outdoor kitchen, shower house and bathrooms; this is an off-grid, sustainable environment (solar power, conscious water use, limited wifi). Artists must be comfortable with close communal living, shared rhythms, a collective food system (primarily vegetarian and pescatarian, sourced from local farmers), and unpaved terrain (which may be difficult for those with mobility impairments). INCLUDED: accommodation, shared indoor/outdoor workspaces, groceries for shared meals and staff support, two on-site CBI artist-faculty, mentorship and feedback, structured and informal group programming, work-in-progress sharing, and photo/video documentation. APPLICATION: online form (no draft saving) with project description (350 words) tied to the chosen track, 1-2 work samples, optional portfolio, artist bio(s) (350 words), and three short-answer questions; you may respond in any language. Apply individually (willing to share), as a duo, or as a trio/small group. Deadline May 24, 2026 (23:59 CET); virtual interviews May 25-29; final notifications June 1. Confirm within 5 days of acceptance with the EUR 100 deposit.

Open call details

item.title: John Hansard Gallery: Open Call for Disabled Artists 2026 (Future Curators)
item.description:
Organisation: John Hansard Gallery, with the University of Southampton (part of the DASH Future Curators Programme)

Location: Within 40 miles of John Hansard Gallery, Southampton, UK

Award: GBP 2,000 artist fee (including up to four meetings with students), plus travel expenses, access costs and a materials budget to be defined. No application fee.

Duration: Project running July 2026 to a February 2027 exhibition

Deadline: Closed 25 May 2026

Open call from John Hansard Gallery for one disabled artist to collaborate with Occupational Therapy and Engineering students at the University of Southampton to make or adapt tools that support their artistic practice. The tools and resulting artwork are displayed in an exhibition at John Hansard Gallery in February 2027, curated by Jess Starns as part of the Future Curators Programme, a disability-justice residency programme for Disabled curators founded by the Disabled artist-led organisation DASH. The work involves collaborating with students in July and August, testing and refining the tools through autumn, and creating artwork for the exhibition. ELIGIBILITY: disabled artists living and working within a 40-mile distance of John Hansard Gallery. Submit an Expression of Interest via the gallery's open-call page; access requirements supported (contact jennifa@jhg.art).

Open call details

item.title: workspacebrussels: Decoratelier Open Call 2026 (Spatial Research Residency)
item.description:
Organisation: workspacebrussels (residency at Decoratelier, Molenbeek)

Location: Decoratelier, Molenbeek, Brussels, Belgium

Award: EUR 5,000 budget (incl. VAT) for the residency, plus a 10x18m warehouse hall, wood and metal workshop access, reusable materials, junior-builder support three days a week, artistic and technical feedback, a possible internal showing, and weekday lunch. No application fee.

Duration: Three-week research residency (5 to 23 October 2026)

Deadline: Closed 31 May 2026

Research-focused residency at Decoratelier in Molenbeek, organised through workspacebrussels, for artists and collectives working with space, scenography, installation, architecture and performative environments. The residency prioritises research and material experimentation over production: applicants propose an investigation into spatial practice, ephemeral architecture or scenographic experimentation connected to the performing arts, engaging directly with the warehouse architecture and reusable materials. ELIGIBILITY: artists or collectives based in Belgium or abroad. NOTE: this is a context-specific research residency and does not provide scenographic support for existing productions. Apply via the workspacebrussels Decoratelier open call.

Open call details

item.title: Artists' Futures Fund: Breakthrough Artist Fellowship 2026-27
item.description:
Organisation: Artists' Futures Fund

Location: England and Wales, UK (in-kind studio space at a partner institution)

Award: GBP 10,000 unrestricted bursary over the 10-month fellowship, plus in-kind studio space, mentorship from a partner institution, professional development, and a co-curated end-of-fellowship group showcase. No application fee.

Duration: 10 months (October 2026 to July 2027)

Deadline: 12 Jun 2026

Needs-based fellowship from the Artists' Futures Fund bridging the gap from graduation to professional visual-arts practice. ELIGIBILITY: recent BA or MA visual-arts graduates (within two years of October 2026) from an AFF partner institution (Cardiff School of Art & Design, Chichester College Group, Liverpool Hope, Loughborough, Manchester Metropolitan, City of Portsmouth College, Sunderland, Swansea College of Art UWTSD), or a relevant Level 3 UAL Diploma/A Levels from City of Portsmouth College; must be eligible to live and work in England or Wales, facing socio-economic, mental and/or physical health barriers, and able to commit fully to the 10 months without additional formal study. Applications open 15 May 2026. Apply via the Artists' Futures Fund support page.

Open call details

item.title: Diriyah Art Futures: Emerging New Media Artists Program 2026/27 (Riyadh)
item.description:
Organisation: Diriyah Art Futures (DAF), in collaboration with Le Fresnoy - Studio National des Arts Contemporains

Location: Diriyah, Saudi Arabia (in-person, intensive on-site programme)

Award: Production budget; access to advanced audio-visual labs, immersive-reality and spatial audio-visual environments; cutting-edge professional equipment; multidisciplinary mentorship from international digital and new-media artists; and a culminating curated group exhibition at DAF in October 2027. Specific stipend amount not stated in the public call. No application fee.

Duration: Approximately one year (programme starts October 2026), with three months of thematic and conceptual seminars followed by a practice-based phase and a final group exhibition in October 2027

Deadline: Closed 31 May 2026

Third cycle of Diriyah Art Futures' Emerging New Media Artists Program, a developmental collaboration with Le Fresnoy - Studio National des Arts Contemporains. The first three months centre on thematic and conceptual exploration through seminars, lectures, screenings and workshops spanning digital technology, socio-cultural dynamics, the environment, more-than-human life and cultural heritage. The programme then moves into a practice-based phase with personalised advisory and access to DAF's labs, encompassing advanced audio-visual techniques, immersive reality and spatial audio-visual environments, concluding with a curated group exhibition at DAF in October 2027. ELIGIBILITY: emerging creative practitioners under 35, at graduate or postgraduate level, with experience in modern media and digital art; open worldwide with a particular focus on practitioners from the Middle East and North Africa. Apply via the linked Ministry of Culture engagement portal.

Open call details

item.title: Tokyo Arts and Space (TOKAS): International Creator Residency 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Tokyo Arts and Space (TOKAS), Tokyo Metropolitan Foundation for History and Culture

Location: TOKAS Residency, 2-14-7 Tatekawa, Sumida-ku, Tokyo, Japan (single room 25 sqm; twin room 49 sqm for duos; shared studios 125 sqm and 48 sqm)

Award: Round-trip economy-class airfare between nearest airport and Tokyo (Narita or Haneda); per diem living expenses of JPY 4,200 per day (excl. tax); fee for work/project of JPY 300,000 (excl. tax); living space (single room) and access to shared studio. Note: 20.42% income tax is deducted from per-diem and project-fee amounts. For duos, airfare is provided for both members but per diem and project fee are paid for one person only. No application fee.

Duration: Three-month residency in one of three slots: May-July 2027, September-November 2027, or January-March 2028 (six creators total, two per slot), concluding with an Open Studio research presentation

Deadline: 16 Jun 2026

Tokyo Arts and Space (TOKAS) International Creator Residency invites practitioners to develop new works and ideas in Tokyo, with research outcomes presented at a closing Open Studio at TOKAS Residency. The programme fosters cross-cultural creative exchange across visual arts (drawing, installation, painting, performance, photography, media art, sculpture, sound art, video, etc.), design and architecture. ELIGIBILITY: creators residing outside Japan with at least five years of experience in visual arts, design or architecture; sufficient English to communicate with residents and TOKAS staff; independent and capable of working and living on one's own; duos eligible if at least one member resides outside Japan and they have completed at least one prior project together; students ineligible except PhD candidates. CONDITIONS: must stay consecutively at TOKAS Residency for the proposed period; create work for the Open Studio and submit a comprehensive project report; cooperate with TOKAS public relations; no commercial or for-profit activities; accommodation is for the participating creator only (no family/partners/friends/guests). Apply via the TOKAS online submission form by 16 June 2026, 18:00 JST: application form (PDF, in English), two letters of recommendation from referees at art and cultural institutions, and a portfolio (max 6 A4 landscape pages, max 10MB, up to 3 projects).

Open call details

Award: $7,500 stipend plus a furnished apartment and access to state-of-the-art photography facilities. The residency concludes with a feature in a special edition of 'Contact Sheet: The Light Work Annual' alongside a commissioned essay. No application fee.

Duration: One-month residency in 2027

Deadline: 01 Jul 2026

Light Work, an independent non-profit founded in 1973, offers a long-running artist-in-residence programme dedicated to the development of experimental and contemporary photographic practices. Over 400 artists have participated in the residency, with many going on to gain international recognition. ELIGIBILITY: artists working in photography or image-based media, from any country and at any career stage. Apply via the Light Work SlideRoom portal.

item.title: BC Arts Council: Individual Arts Grants - Media Artists 2026
item.description:
Organisation: BC Arts Council (Province of British Columbia)

Location: British Columbia, Canada (provincial council; BC residency typically required)

Award: Up to $25,000 CAD per project. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: 17 Jun 2026

BC Arts Council's Individual Arts Grants stream for independent media artists working in moving image (film, video, narrative, experimental, expanded cinema, installation), audio/sound art (sound sculptures, installations, sound walks, gallery presentations) or new media and digital arts (interactive installations, immersive environments, web-based art, ICT). ELIGIBILITY: independent individual media artists (not groups or organisations); projects must be independent of commercial industries. As a provincial arts council, BC Arts Council typically requires applicants to be residents of British Columbia - confirm current residency requirements on the linked page before applying. NOTE: registration must be completed by 17 June 2026 at 23:59 PT, ahead of the 24 June application deadline. Applications via the SmartSimple Grant Management System. item.title: The Lab: Living Wage Residencies 2026-27 (San Francisco)
item.description:
Organisation: The Lab

Location: San Francisco Bay Area, California, USA (eligibility restricted to artists based in the Bay Area; significant in-person access to The Lab's space in the Mission District is required)

Award: USD 17,530 artist fee per resident (commensurate with three months of work at a San Francisco Bay Area living wage per MIT's Living Wage Calculator) plus USD 5,000 for collaborators and materials. Three months of rehearsal/workshop access to The Lab's space, plus two weeks of continuous use of the exhibition and performance spaces for the residency outcome. Curatorial and marketing support, outside studio visits, documentation and publicity. Stage-2 finalists receive an additional USD 250 stipend to develop their full proposal. No application fee.

Duration: Residencies take place between July 2026 and June 2027

Deadline: Closed 31 May 2026

The Lab's flagship Living Wage Residency, a substantial in-person residency in San Francisco's Mission District for artists working in any media and discipline. Since 2014 the programme has directed over USD 1 million to artists, with artist fees representing nearly 30% of The Lab's average operating budget. The Lab's history is as a site for experimental and interdisciplinary work spanning performance, exhibition, installation, sound and experimental music, writing, institutional critique, social practice, parades, feasts and further disciplines yet to be defined. The Lab's mission centres traditionally underrepresented artists, particularly those identifying as BIPOC, LGBTQ+, women, disabled and/or from economically marginalised backgrounds. ELIGIBILITY: artists based in the San Francisco Bay Area (tight geographic restriction; in-person access to The Lab's space is central to the residency). SUPPORT per residency: USD 17,530 artist fee (3 months at a Bay Area living wage per MIT's Living Wage Calculator); USD 5,000 for collaborators and materials; three months of rehearsal/workshop access to The Lab's space (as available around other programming); two weeks of continuous use of the exhibition and performance spaces; curatorial and marketing support, outside studio visits, documentation and publicity. PROCESS: Stage 1 application (CV, 3 work links, brief project description and resource plan) due 31 May 2026; Stage 2 invites up to 12 selected artists to develop a full proposal with a USD 250 stipend (Stage 2 deadline TBD). Reviewed by a diverse panel of professional artists and curators plus The Lab's Board. Outside funding additionally secured for a proposal is not factored into the decision, but The Lab may help identify further sources. Supported by the San Francisco Arts Commission.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.description:

Location: Netherlands

Award: EUR 50,000 to EUR 125,000 per year for the 2027 and 2028 calendar years

Duration: Two years, 2027 and 2028

Deadline: 15 Jun 2026

Two-year activity-programme funding from the Creative Industries Fund NL for organisations active in design, digital culture or architecture. Supports a coherent multi-year programme of activities running across 2027 and 2028 (rather than single projects). The funding underwrites a portion of the organisation's annual programme budget, giving recipients planning stability over the two-year period. Application window 13 May - 15 June 2026. EUR 50,000 to EUR 125,000 per year per organisation, depending on the scale and ambition of the proposed programme. ELIGIBILITY: cultural and creative-industries organisations established in the Netherlands working in design, digital culture or architecture; a distinct scheme from the Fund's annual project grants, festival rounds and voucher schemes.

item.title: Stroom Den Haag: PRO Grants 2026 (Research / Projects / Programmes / Investment)
item.description:
Organisation: Stroom Den Haag

Location: The Hague, Netherlands (eligibility restricted to professional visual artists registered with Stroom in the municipality of The Hague)

Award: Four schemes - PRO Research, PRO Projects, PRO Programmes, and PRO Investment - with amounts typically EUR 2,500 to EUR 20,000+ depending on scheme and proposal.

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: Closed 31 May 2026

Stroom Den Haag's PRO grants support professional visual artists registered with Stroom in The Hague through four schemes: PRO Research (research and development for new work), PRO Projects (project realisation), PRO Programmes (multi-stage artistic programmes spanning research, production and presentation), and PRO Investment (investment in artistic practice, e.g. equipment, studio, professional development). Three remaining 2026 application rounds: 31 May, 6 September and 25 October 2026. ELIGIBILITY: professional visual artists registered with Stroom in the municipality of The Hague (tightly geographic). Amounts vary by scheme and proposal, typically EUR 2,500 to EUR 20,000+.
